**Wolli Creek** ([/ˈwɒlaɪ/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English")) is an urban [watercourse](/wiki/Perennial_stream "Perennial stream") of the [Cooks River](/wiki/Cooks_River "Cooks River") catchment located in the [southern](/wiki/Southern_Sydney "Southern Sydney") suburbs of [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney"), in [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia").

## Course and features

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Wolli_Creek&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Course and features")]

Wolli Creek rises south of [Narwee](/wiki/Narwee "Narwee"), within Beverly Hills Park, [Beverly Hills](/wiki/Beverly_Hills,_New_South_Wales "Beverly Hills, New South Wales"). The creek flows generally east northeast through Wolli Creek Valley and [Wolli Creek Regional Park](/wiki/Wolli_Creek_Regional_Park "Wolli Creek Regional Park"), joined by its major [tributary](/wiki/Tributary "Tributary"), [Bardwell Creek](/wiki/Bardwell_Creek "Bardwell Creek"), before reaching its [confluence](/wiki/Confluence "Confluence") with the Cooks River near [Arncliffe](/wiki/Arncliffe,_New_South_Wales "Arncliffe, New South Wales") and [Tempe](/wiki/Tempe,_New_South_Wales "Tempe, New South Wales").[[1]](#cite_note-gnb-1) The creek is a lined channel between Kingsgrove Road, [Kingsgrove](/wiki/Kingsgrove "Kingsgrove") and Bexley Road, [Bexley North](/wiki/Bexley_North "Bexley North") where it then enters the Wolli Creek Valley. The sub-catchment area of the creek is 22 square kilometres (8.5 sq mi).

### Nature conservation value

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Wolli_Creek&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Nature conservation value")]

Adjacent to Wolli Creek, within the Wolli Creek Valley, is Wolli Creek Regional Park, a planned 50 hectares (120 acres) [nature reserve](/wiki/Nature_reserve "Nature reserve")[[2]](#cite_note-WCPS-Regional_park-2) of native bushland and public reserves that was announced by the [NSW Government](/wiki/Government_of_New_South_Wales "Government of New South Wales") in 1998 as a result of sustained community campaigning for the area to be preserved and for the [M5 East Freeway](/wiki/M5_Motorway_(Sydney)#M5_East "M5 Motorway (Sydney)") to go underground.[[2]](#cite_note-WCPS-Regional_park-2) Whilst some of the park has been formed and management handed over from [local government authorities](/wiki/Local_government_in_Australia "Local government in Australia") to the [NSW National Parks & Wildlife Service](/wiki/NSW_National_Parks_%26_Wildlife_Service "NSW National Parks & Wildlife Service"), including the 8.9 hectares (22 acres) Girrahween Park[[2]](#cite_note-WCPS-Regional_park-2) and some privately held land that was [compulsorily acquired](/wiki/Eminent_domain "Eminent domain"),[[3]](#cite_note-3) some areas of the originally planned park remain in the hands of government agencies including [Sydney Water](/wiki/Sydney_Water "Sydney Water") and [Roads & Maritime Services](/wiki/Roads_%26_Maritime_Services "Roads & Maritime Services").[[2]](#cite_note-WCPS-Regional_park-2)

When complete, the planned nature reserve offers easy public transport access, family picnic areas, extensive views and bushland, rugged sandstone escarpments with walking tracks, a mixture of parkland, heathland, and woodland forest, and great birdwatching in close proximity to heavily developed residential and industrial landscape.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

#### Wildlife

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Wolli_Creek&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Wildlife")]

##### Bird observation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Wolli_Creek&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Bird observation")]

The diversity of habitat and strategic location of the Wolli Creek Valley are reflected in the wide range of bird species to be found adjacent to the creek. Approximately 175 species of birds have been recorded in the area between 1940 and 1999.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

##### Fish

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Wolli_Creek&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Fish")]

Several species of freshwater and saltwater fish can be found in the Wolli Creek in areas above and below the [fish ladder](/wiki/Fish_ladder "Fish ladder"). The fish ladder, or "rock-ramp fishway"[[6]](#cite_note-6) was constructed to enable fish to bypass the [weir](/wiki/Weir "Weir")[[7]](#cite_note-7) at Henderson St, Turrella, allowing migration to other areas of the creek.

#### Coastal saltmarsh

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Wolli_Creek&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Coastal saltmarsh")]

The lower reaches – the tidal part – of Wolli Creek are home to a plant community known as [saltmarsh](/wiki/Saltmarsh "Saltmarsh"),[[8]](#cite_note-Brown-8) or coastal saltmarsh.[[9]](#cite_note-DPI-9) The plant community includes various salinity-tolerant species such as Knobby Club-rush (*[Ficinia nodosa](/wiki/Ficinia_nodosa "Ficinia nodosa")*), Samphire (*[Sarcocornia quinqueflora](/wiki/Sarcocornia_quinqueflora "Sarcocornia quinqueflora")*), Sea Rush (*[Juncus kraussii](/wiki/Juncus_kraussii "Juncus kraussii")*), Seablite (*[Suaeda australis](/wiki/Suaeda_australis "Suaeda australis")*), Streaked Arrowgrass (*[Triglochin striata](/wiki/Triglochin_striata "Triglochin striata")*) and others.[[9]](#cite_note-DPI-9) Coastal saltmarsh has been recognised as a [carbon sink](/wiki/Carbon_sink "Carbon sink") as well as a filter of nutrients maintaining water quality and is listed as an Endangered Ecological Community under the [Threatened Species Conservation Act 1995](http://www.environment.nsw.gov.au/threatenedspecies/tscact.htm).[[9]](#cite_note-DPI-9)
